quickStart.title: Get running in 5 minutes
steps: Run: hermes init in your project directory
Edit the generated hermes.config.json with your API key and model preferences
Hermes executes autonomously — check hermes.log for what it did

dailyWorkflows.title: One-shot task execution
dailyWorkflows.description: Run a single focused task from the CLI.
dailyWorkflows.command: hermes 'fix all TypeScript errors in src/'

dailyWorkflows.title: Interactive mode
dailyWorkflows.description: Run Hermes in a REPL-style session for iterative work.

dailyWorkflows.title: Dry run — see the plan without executing
dailyWorkflows.description: Generate an execution plan without touching any files.

dailyWorkflows.title: Run from a task file
dailyWorkflows.description: Put complex tasks in a markdown file and pass it as input.

dailyWorkflows.title: Limit scope to specific paths
dailyWorkflows.description: Restrict what files Hermes can read and edit.

dailyWorkflows.title: Check what Hermes did
dailyWorkflows.description: Review the detailed execution log after a task.

powerMoves.title: Chain tasks with a pipeline file
powerMoves.description: Define a sequence of tasks that run in order, with outputs piped as inputs.
powerMoves.why: Automate multi-step workflows that normally require manual handoffs.

powerMoves.title: Use hermes.context.md for persistent instructions
powerMoves.description: Add a context file at project root with architecture notes and rules.
powerMoves.why: Hermes injects this into every task's context — better output without repeating yourself.

powerMoves.title: Capture structured output
powerMoves.description: Ask Hermes to output JSON you can pipe into another tool.
powerMoves.command: hermes --output-json 'list all API endpoints as JSON'
powerMoves.why: Useful for generating data, configs, or feeding into scripts.

powerMoves.title: Parallel task execution
powerMoves.why: Speeds up batch work — each task gets its own context.

gotchas.problem: Hermes edits files outside the expected scope
gotchas.solution: Always use --scope to restrict file access. Or set allowedPaths in hermes.config.json.
Set HERMES_API_KEY env var or add it to hermes.config.json. Check with hermes config show.
Add --max-steps 20 to cap iterations. Check hermes.log to see where it looped.
Must be at project root (where you ran hermes init). Check hermes config show for contextPath.

cliFlags.description: Show the execution plan without modifying any files.
cliFlags.useCase: Validate the plan before committing to changes.

cliFlags.description: Start an interactive session with back-and-forth conversation.
Restrict file access to a specific path. Hermes cannot read or edit outside this path.
cliFlags.example: hermes --scope src/api/ 'add input validation to all endpoints'
cliFlags.useCase: Focused tasks where you want to prevent accidental edits to other parts.

cliFlags.description: Read the task description from a file (markdown or plain text).
cliFlags.useCase: Complex tasks that are easier to write in a file than inline.

cliFlags.description: Maximum number of agent iterations before stopping.
Override the model for this task.
cliFlags.useCase: Use a stronger model for complex tasks, cheaper one for quick fixes.

cliFlags.description: Ask Hermes to return structured JSON output.
cliFlags.useCase: Piping Hermes output into scripts or other tools.
Batch parallel execution of independent tasks.

config.description: API key for the configured model provider.
config.recommended: Use env var in production. Store in hermes.config.json for local dev only.

config.description: Default model to use for all tasks.
config.recommended: Sonnet for most tasks. Opus for long autonomous runs.

config.description: Path to the context file injected into every task.
config.recommended: Create this file with project conventions and architecture notes.

config.description: Array of paths Hermes is permitted to read and edit. Empty = no restriction.
config.recommended: Set to your source directories in production deployments.

config.description: Default maximum agent iterations per task.
config.recommended: Lower to 20-30 for most tasks to prevent runaway loops.

proTips: Always run --dry-run first on unfamiliar tasks to validate the plan.
Create hermes.context.md immediately on any new project.
Use --scope on every task that targets a specific module.
For long tasks, break them into a --pipeline of smaller steps.
Check hermes.log after every run — it shows exactly what the agent decided and why.
Set maxSteps low by default and increase only when needed.
